title: AgentOrchestrationNats
description: NATS subjects, queue groups, and JetStream streams used by the agent thread orchestration plane: REST API producer, queue consumer, idle reaper, cron, and per-thread workers.

$dd:nats.summary: Per-thread task dispatch and orchestrator wakeup signals. See remote/deployments/nats/future.md for the full design.

subjects.name: ThreadTasks
subjects.description: Per-thread task queue. JetStream-backed (DD_REMOTE_TASKS). Producers publish per-thread; consumers either subscribe to the exact subject (the worker for that thread) or to the wildcard via a queue group (the preparer).

params.name: thread_id
params.description: Thread UUID (dashes ok; NATS treats hyphens as a single token).

subjects.name: ThreadControl
subjects.description: Per-thread control-plane commands (scale, evict, reload). Short-retention JetStream control stream.

subjects.name: ThreadEvents
subjects.description: Per-thread worker events (progress, status, output). Longer-retention JetStream events stream.

subjects.name: OrchestratorWakeup
subjects.description: Wakeup signal published whenever a new task is enqueued for a thread, so the orchestrator can prepare/scale the matching worker deployment without polling.
subjects.subject: dd.remote.orchestrator.wakeup

subjects.name: CronPrompts
subjects.description: Scheduled prompts published by dd-chron-service. A producer converts these into normal per-thread task messages so cron doesn't bypass idempotency or affinity.
subjects.subject: dd.remote.cron.prompts

queueGroups.name: ThreadPreparerQueueGroup
queueGroups.description: Shared queue group used by dd-remote-queue-consumer replicas so each task is only prepared once.

streams.name: DD_REMOTE_TASKS
streams.description: JetStream file storage, explicit ack, message dedupe by Nats-Msg-Id ('remote-task:<taskId>'). Postgres remains the real idempotency guard.

streams.name: DD_REMOTE_EVENTS
streams.description: Longer-retention event stream for replay/debugging, mirrored into Postgres.
